notes: United Nations specialized agency for ICT, three sectors: ITU-T (Standardization), ITU-R (Radiocommunication), ITU-D (Development). ITU-T Recommendations are FREE TO DOWNLOAD from itu.int — but ITU retains copyright and explicit permission is required for redistribution / republication (see https://www.itu.int/en/Pages/copyright.aspx). Same posture as AICPA TSC: access is free, redistribution is not granted by default. Notable security recommendations: X.805 (security architecture), X.1051 (info security management for telecom orgs — basis for ISO/IEC 27011), X.509 (certificate format), X.1500-series (cybersecurity exchange formats).

match_nodes.description: ITU-T X.805 — Security architecture for end-to-end communications

data.official_name: ITU-T X.805 - Security architecture for systems providing end-to-end communications
data.notes: Free to download from itu.int. Redistribution requires ITU permission. Defines a layered/planes/dimensions security architecture model widely cited in telecom and network security work.

match_nodes.description: ITU-T X.1051 — Information security management for telecommunications organizations

data.official_name: ITU-T X.1051 - Information security management guidelines for telecommunications organizations based on ISO/IEC 27002
data.notes: Free to download from itu.int. Redistribution requires ITU permission. ITU-T's ISMS guidance for telecom organizations; technical basis for ISO/IEC 27011.
